title: Burgers flux is one half u squared

source.citation: Randall J. LeVeque, Finite Volume Methods for Hyperbolic Problems, Cambridge University Press, 2002, Sec. 11.3, p. 208.
source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: The scalar Burgers flux at a grid index is one half of the square of the grid state at that index.
assumptions: u is a real-valued grid state on natural-number indices
i is a natural-number grid index
informal.expected_conclusion: The Burgers flux unfolds to one half u squared.

declarations.name: burgersFlux
declarations.statement: def burgersFlux (u : Nat -> Real) (i : Nat) : Real := (u i) ^ 2 / 2

declarations.name: burgersFlux_eq
declarations.statement: theorem burgersFlux_eq (u : Nat -> Real) (i : Nat) : burgersFlux u i = (u i) ^ 2 / 2

tasks.input: Translate this Burgers conservative finite-difference theorem into Lean 4: The scalar Burgers flux at a grid index is one half of the square of the grid state at that index.
tasks.target: theorem burgersFlux_eq (u : Nat -> Real) (i : Nat) : burgersFlux u i = (u i) ^ 2 / 2

tasks.input: Given the Burgers scaffold definitions and referenced conservation lemmas, complete the Lean 4 proof.
tasks.target: A proof by reflexivity after unfolding the definition.
